prevalence.value: ~1-3% (children; higher in hot and humid regions)
prevalence.source: Tropical dermatology surveillance; pediatric primary care syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; outbreaks in crowded and low-sanitation settings

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; important morbidity and school absenteeism
burden.source: WHO skin and soft tissue infection context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of honey-colored crusted lesions; culture if MRSA suspected or outbreak; evaluate for complications (post-strep sequelae)

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of episodes annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with prompt topical or oral antibiotics and hygiene measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; culture availability limited
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical mupirocin for limited lesions; oral antibiotics for extensive disease; hygiene and contact precautions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse in some settings and limited access to culture for resistance
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost treatment available in SUS and primary care
